askBeynd.systemPromptBase:
You are Beynd, a calm personal financial coach.

You receive a short summary the user keeps on this device. Bank connection is not live yet. This is coaching, not regulated financial advice.

The summary has monthly figures from what they entered, including priority steps for this month. It may also include a balance they added for a sense-check.

Use only numbers from the summary. Do not invent figures.

If they ask whether they can afford something and no balance is listed this month, or the summary says Worth another look or Go gently here, lead with what their monthly picture shows. Mention once that refreshing their balance would sharpen the answer. Then continue with useful guidance. Do not tell them they can afford it as fact.

If a balance is on file and the summary says Looks steady, you may compare both views in plain language. Stay practical and warm. Do not sound like a bank or a guarantee.

Do not repeat caveats unnecessarily. Explain them once clearly, then continue naturally.

Be specific with their numbers. Use two or three sentences unless more detail clearly helps. Be honest without shaming. Stay calm and direct.
askBeynd.subIntro: Choose a prompt or type your own question.
askBeynd.firstRunIntro: Ask Beynd about your plan, goals, or financial decisions. Type a question, choose an option, or follow a coaching hint when you want a steadier next step.

modal.title: Ask Beynd
modal.caption: Ask from your plan and the picture you have built in Beynd. Bank connection is not live yet.
modal.inputPlaceholder: Ask about your plan, money, or next move
modal.privacyFoot: When you ask, Beynd sends a short financial summary to answer your question. Your plan stays on this device, and this chat is not stored by Beynd.
modal.fallbackReply: Sorry, I could not answer that.
modal.unavailableError: Ask Beynd is unavailable right now. Your plan still works.

reflection.intro: Ask about what you just read on Reflection, or edit the question below.
reflection.subIntro: Nothing sends until you tap Ask.
starters: What should I watch this month?
How does my plan fit what I shared?
What can wait for now?
stabilityStarters: What needs me first this month?

modifierStarters.default: What should I keep in mind this month?
modifierStarters.worriedOnly: What’s worth not rushing right now?

neutral.intro: I’m ready when you are. Ask about your plan, money, or next move.
starters: How should I prioritise this month?
Am I on track?
What’s costing me the most in interest?
What does my plan suggest this month?

second_opinion.intro: If you want to think it through before acting, ask here.
starters: What needs me first this month?
What can safely wait?
Talk me through my plan’s first step.

pressure_test.intro: Want to sense-check this move before you schedule it?
starters: What would make this payment feel safer?
What trade-offs should I consider?
What does my plan assume I still have room for?

reality_mismatch.intro: Your plan and balance check tell slightly different stories. I can help you think through that.
starters: Help me understand this gap.
What might have changed since my balance check?
Should I adjust anything, or just watch for now?

still_deciding.intro: No rush. I can help you sort the next useful step.
starters: What would help me decide?
What’s the smallest useful next step?
What should I check before I act?

explain_plan.intro: I can walk through what your plan is suggesting and why.
starters: Walk me through this month’s plan.
What does my plan suggest first?
Why is this the next step?

after_action.intro: You’ve made progress. Ask if you want help choosing what’s next.
starters: What should I watch for next?
What’s a sensible next move from here?
How should I keep this momentum?

sinceLastVisit.updated: You made updates recently. Your plan has adjusted.
sinceLastVisit.noIncomeUpdated: You made updates recently. Your snapshot reflects those changes.
sinceLastVisit.stale: It’s been a few days. Your plan still reflects your last setup.
sinceLastVisit.noIncomeStale: It’s been a few days. Your last setup is still what we’re showing.
sinceLastVisit.noChange: No major changes since your last visit.

consistency.title: Good rhythm
consistency.goalFollowThrough: You have kept showing up for your goal. That steady rhythm matters.
consistency.contributionFollowThrough: You have followed through more than once recently. That kind of consistency matters.
consistency.consistentWeeklyUse: You have been keeping up with your plan this week. Keep going.

dependants.body: Since this income supports others too, keeping some room may be the safer move.

protectFlexibility.default: Keep some room before adding new commitments.
protectFlexibility.monthContext: This month already has something on your mind. Keep flexibility before adding more.

reviewFirst.default: Today may need a slower pace. Review what has changed before adding anything new.
reviewFirst.realityGap: Today may need a slower pace. Review what has changed before adding anything new.

cash_pressure_home.title: Cash flow
variants: You’re over budget this month — rebalance spending or due dates.
Cash flow is negative right now — trim or reschedule before adding new commitments.
This month is tight — stabilise cash before chasing growth moves.
cash_pressure_home.explain: Scheduled payments and expenses count against income, so a negative left-this-month means the plan reads strained for this month.

spending_update.title: Spending
variants: Left this month shifted after that spending change.
Spending moved — quick check on left this month.
Spending totals changed — plan room moved with them.
spending_update.strongMessage: Spending shifted — left this month reads tighter on your plan.
spending_update.mildMessage: Spending nudged up — left this month moved.
spending_update.easedMessage: Spending eased a little — a bit more left this month.
spending_update.explain: Expenses count toward monthly outflows, so they change left this month on your plan until the month resets.

debt_action_home.title: Debt
variants: Debt moved — the loudest APR still deserves the first reply.
Paydown progress — the fastest win is usually the highest-interest balance.
Balance shifted — shrinking the costliest line item frees the rest of your plan.
debt_action_home.explain: Because interest accrues on balances, reducing the highest-APR debt cuts monthly drag the fastest.

goal_action_home.title: Goals
variants: Goal savings moved — consistency beats occasional big jumps.
Progress landed — small monthly adds keep dates realistic.
You topped up a goal — steady pace is what makes targets stick.
goal_action_home.explain: Because goals are funded over time, regular contributions matter more than one-off spikes.

invest_action_home.title: Investing
variants: Investments moved — keep steering with your plan, not the headlines.
Portfolio shifted — consistent contributions matter more than timing.
You invested recently — repetition is how compounding notices you.
invest_action_home.explain: Because compounding needs time and repetition, regular investing supports long-term growth plans.

income_action_home.title: Income
income_action_home.message: Income changed — revisit what’s realistic for debt, goals, and investing.
income_action_home.explain: Because income sets the ceiling for monthly flow, updates should refresh how aggressive your plan can be.

debt_habit_gap.title: Debt pattern
debt_habit_gap.message: You typically reduce your debt steadily — continuing that pattern will keep interest down.

invest_habit_gap.title: Investing rhythm
invest_habit_gap.message: You usually invest regularly, but it has not happened recently — staying consistent builds momentum.

goal_habit_gap.title: Goal funding
goal_habit_gap.message: You have been consistent with your goals before — picking it back up keeps you on track.

investHarder.message: Investing suggestions have been harder to act on — starting smaller may help.

goalPartial.message: You tend to make partial progress on goals — that still counts, but a little more would get you back on pace.

debtMomentum.message: You usually follow through on debt actions — this is a good place to keep momentum.

debt_payment.title: Reduce high-interest debt
debt_payment.sub: This reduces your highest interest cost first.

buffer_contribution.title: Build your safety buffer
buffer_contribution.sub: Creates a buffer for unexpected expenses.

investment_contribution.title: Start investing
investment_contribution.sub: Even small amounts compound over time.

adaptiveSubtitles.debt_payment: Start with a smaller extra payment this month — your plan still targets the full amount when you are ready.
adaptiveSubtitles.goal_contribution: A smaller contribution still keeps progress moving — the full suggestion is on Plan when you want it.
adaptiveSubtitles.investment_contribution: Start smaller on the recurring amount — match the full plan when ready.
adaptiveSubtitles.default: Even a modest buffer step helps — build toward the full plan suggestion over time.
